title: The hidden cost of running AI in production (it's not what you think)
post:
Everyone talks about AI inference costs. The hidden cost nobody mentions: maintenance.

Models drift. User behavior shifts. Edge cases emerge. Your 'working' AI feature needs constant babysitting.

In our production systems, we spend 3x more on AI maintenance than initial deployment:
- Monitoring for drift and degradation
- Updating training data
- Retraining and redeploying
- Handling failure modes nobody anticipated

The real cost of AI isn't the API bill. It's the human expertise required to keep it working.

Before you deploy AI, ask: who's going to maintain this when it breaks at 2am?

How are you handling AI maintenance costs?
